headline: Northrop Grumman Releases Second Quarter 2026 Financial Results
summary: FALLS CHURCH, Va., July 21, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Northrop Grumman Corporation (NYSE: NOC) has released its second quarter 2026 financial results. headline: Northrop Grumman’s (NYSE:NOC) Q2 CY2026 Sales Beat Estimates
summary: Security and aerospace company Northrop Grumman (NYSE:NOC) beat Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q2 CY2026, with sales up 5.1% year on year to $10.88 billion. The company expects the full year’s revenue to be around $44 billion, close to analysts’ estimates. Its GAAP profit of $7.68 per share was 12.6% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: Northrop Grumman’s (NYSE:NOC) Q2 CY2026 Sales Beat Estimates
content.summary: Security and aerospace company Northrop Grumman (NYSE:NOC) beat Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q2 CY2026, with sales up 5.1% year on year to $10.88 billion. The company expects the full year’s revenue to be around $44 billion, close to analysts’ estimates. Its GAAP profit of $7.68 per share was 12.6% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: What Northrop Grumman (NOC)'s Roy Sentinel Campus Expansion Means For Shareholders
summary: Earlier this month, Northrop Grumman broke ground on a new Legacy Building at its Roy Innovation Center in Utah, expanding the Sentinel ICBM-focused campus to six buildings and more than 1,100,000 square feet of office space with capacity for over 5,000 employees. This expansion, backed by multi-billion dollar infrastructure and R&D spending and thousands of supported jobs statewide, underlines Northrop Grumman’s deepening role in long-cycle U.S. strategic deterrence and advanced aerospace...

headline: Why Northrop Grumman (NOC) Stock Is Down Today
summary: Shares of aerospace and defense company Northrop Grumman (NYSE: NOC) fell 5.8% in the pre-market session after the company reported mixed second-quarter financial results, revealing margin compression and a weak earnings outlook that unsettled investors. On the surface, the headline numbers were strong. Earnings per share of $7.68 and revenue of $10.88 billion both beat Wall Street's estimates, and organic revenue rose 7% year on year. The company also pushed its backlog to $105 billion, up 17%

content.title: Why Northrop Grumman (NOC) Stock Is Down Today
content.summary: Shares of aerospace and defense company Northrop Grumman (NYSE: NOC) fell 5.8% in the pre-market session after the company reported mixed second-quarter financial results, revealing margin compression and a weak earnings outlook that unsettled investors. On the surface, the headline numbers were strong. Earnings per share of $7.68 and revenue of $10.88 billion both beat Wall Street's estimates, and organic revenue rose 7% year on year. The company also pushed its backlog to $105 billion, up 17%
